curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.gradeLevel: Class XI / Class XII / 12th Grade / Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 28 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Apply fundamental and derived SI units for physical quantities.
Determine appropriate significant figures in measurements.
Calculate and express uncertainty in experimental results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships.
Use differentiation and integration concepts to describe linear motion.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity.
Analyze motion using velocity-time and position-time graphs.
Apply graphical and calculus methods for uniformly accelerated motion.
Perform addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication of vectors.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components using unit vectors.
Calculate scalar and vector products for physical quantities.
Analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion in a plane.

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body and to measure internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using Vernier Callipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of a given wire and thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine volume of an irregular lamina using screw gauge.
To determine radius of curvature of a given spherical surface by a spherometer.
To determine the mass of two different objects using a beam balance.
To find the weight of a given body using parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum, plot its graph and use it to find the effective length of second's pendulum.
To study variation of time period of a simple pendulum of a given length by taking bobs of same size but different masses and interpret the result.
To study the relationship between force of limiting friction and normal reaction and to find the co- efficient of friction between a block and a horizontal surface.

To find the downward force, along an inclined plane, acting on a roller due to gravitational pull of the earth and study its relationship with the angle of inclination θ by plotting graph between force and Sinθ.
To determine Young's modulus of elasticity of the material of a given wire.
To find the force constant of a helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the variation in volume with pressure for a sample of air at constant temperature by plotting graphs between P and V, and between P and 1/V.
practicals.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

practicals.title: To determine the surface tension of water by capillary rise method.
To determine the coefficient of viscosity of a given viscous liquid by measuring terminal velocity of a given spherical body.
To study the relationship between the temperature of a hot body and time by plotting a cooling curve.
To determine specific heat capacity of a given solid by method of mixtures.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension for constant frequency using sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by two resonance positions.
To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.

To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v.
To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body using vernier calipers.
To measure the internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using vernier calipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of given wire using screw gauge.
To measure thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine the mass of a given object using a beam balance.
To find the weight of given body using the parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum plot L-T and graphs. Hence find the effective length of second's pendulum using appropriate length values.
To find the force constant of given helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.

(i) To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using a sonometer. (ii) To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension, for constant frequency, using a sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air, at room temperature, using a resonance tube, by observing the two resonance positions.
To determine the resistance per cm of a given wire by plotting a graph between voltage and current.
To verify the laws of combination (series/parallel combination) of resistances by Ohm's law.
To find the resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using a meter bridge.
To determine the resistance of a galvanometer by half deflection method.
To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items.

To observe the difference between (i) a convex lens and a concave lens (ii) a convex mirror and a concave mirror and to estimate the likely difference between the power of two given convex /concave lenses.
To design an inductor coil and to know the effect of (i) change in the number of turns (ii) Introduction of ferromagnetic material as its core material on the inductance of the coil.
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

practicals.title: To design a (i) step up (ii) step down transformer on a given core and know the relation between its input and output voltages.
practicals.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

activities.title: To make a paper scale of given least count, e.g., 0.2cm, 0.5 cm.
To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.
To plot a graph for a given set of data, with proper choice of scales and error bars.
To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.
To note the change in level of liquid in a container on heating and interpret the observations.
To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.
To study the factors affecting the rate of loss of heat of a liquid.
To study the effect of load on depression of a suitably clamped metre scale loaded at (i) its end (ii) in the middle.
To observe the decrease in pressure with increase in velocity of a fluid.
To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.
To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order.
To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.

To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.
To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.
To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.

To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.
projects.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

marks_distribution: Class XI/XII Theory: Max Marks 70, Time 3 hours
Class XI/XII Practical: Max Marks 30, Time 2-3 hours (Visually Impaired: 2 hours)

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way.

One Activity from any section (3 Marks)

Viva on experiments, activities and project

Variations in current flowing in circuit containing LDR due to variation in power/distance of lamp
Find refractive indices of water/oil using plane mirror and lens
Investigate relation between output/input voltage and turns ratio in transformer
Dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism
Estimate charge induced on Styrofoam balls using Coulomb's law
Study factor on which self-inductance depends
Study earth's magnetic field using compass needle-bar magnet

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body and to measure internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using Vernier Callipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of a given wire and thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine volume of an irregular lamina using screw gauge.
To determine radius of curvature of a given spherical surface by a spherometer.
To determine the mass of two different objects using a beam balance.
To find the weight of a given body using parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum, plot its graph and use it to find the effective length of second's pendulum.
To study variation of time period of a simple pendulum of a given length by taking bobs of same size but different masses and interpret the result.
To study the relationship between force of limiting friction and normal reaction and to find the co- efficient of friction between a block and a horizontal surface.

To determine Young's modulus of elasticity of the material of a given wire.
To find the force constant of a helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the variation in volume with pressure for a sample of air at constant temperature by plotting graphs between P and V, and between P and 1/V.

practicals.title: To determine the surface tension of water by capillary rise method.
To determine the coefficient of viscosity of a given viscous liquid by measuring terminal velocity of a given spherical body.
To study the relationship between the temperature of a hot body and time by plotting a cooling curve.
To determine specific heat capacity of a given solid by method of mixtures.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension for constant frequency using sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by two resonance positions.
To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.

To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v.
To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body using vernier calipers.
To measure the internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using vernier calipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of given wire using screw gauge.
To measure thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine the mass of a given object using a beam balance.
To find the weight of given body using the parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum plot L-T and graphs. Hence find the effective length of second's pendulum using appropriate length values.
To find the force constant of given helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.

(i) To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using a sonometer. (ii) To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension, for constant frequency, using a sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air, at room temperature, using a resonance tube, by observing the two resonance positions.
To determine the resistance per cm of a given wire by plotting a graph between voltage and current.
To verify the laws of combination (series/parallel combination) of resistances by Ohm's law.
To find the resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using a meter bridge.
To determine the resistance of a galvanometer by half deflection method.
To identify a resistor, capacitor, inductor and diode from a mixed collection of such items.

To observe the difference between (i) a convex lens and a concave lens (ii) a convex mirror and a concave mirror and to estimate the likely difference between the power of two given convex /concave lenses.
To design an inductor coil and to know the effect of (i) change in the number of turns (ii) Introduction of ferromagnetic material as its core material on the inductance of the coil.

question_paper_design.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts, and answers.
Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions, and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts; Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas, or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way.

One Activity from any section (3 Marks)

components.component: Two experiments one from each section
Viva on experiments, activities and project

components.description: At least five experiments recorded and signed by internal examiner.

project_assessment.description: Report of the project carried out by students.
examples: Study various factors on which internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends
Variations in current flowing in circuit containing LDR due to variation in power/distance of lamp
Find refractive indices of water/oil using plane mirror and lens
Investigate relation between output/input voltage and turns ratio in transformer
Dependence of angle of deviation on angle of incidence using hollow prism
Estimate charge induced on Styrofoam balls using Coulomb's law
Study factor on which self-inductance depends
Study earth's magnetic field using compass needle-bar magnet

project_assessment.description: Maintain practical file with at least five experiments recorded.

competency_groups.title: Units and Measurements Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, uncertainty determination, dimensional analysis, and their applications.
competencies: Apply fundamental and derived SI units for physical quantities.
Determine appropriate significant figures in measurements.
Calculate and express uncertainty in experimental results.
Perform dimensional analysis to verify equations and derive relationships.

competency_groups.title: Motion in a Straight Line Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to describing motion using calculus, analyzing uniform/non-uniform motion, interpreting graphs, and applying equations of uniformly accelerated motion.
competencies: Use differentiation and integration concepts to describe linear motion.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity.
Analyze motion using velocity-time and position-time graphs.
Apply graphical and calculus methods for uniformly accelerated motion.

competency_groups.title: Motion in a Plane Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector operations, resolution of vectors, and analyzing projectile and circular motion.
competencies: Perform addition, subtraction, and scalar multiplication of vectors.
Resolve vectors into rectangular components using unit vectors.
Calculate scalar and vector products for physical quantities.
Analyze projectile motion and uniform circular motion in a plane.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Newton's laws, momentum conservation, impulse, force equilibrium, friction types, and centripetal dynamics.
competencies: Explain inertia and apply Newton's first law in various contexts.
Relate force, mass, acceleration via Newton's second law and calculate impulse.
Apply the principle of conservation of linear momentum derived from Newton's third law.
Analyze equilibrium conditions involving static/kinetic friction and centripetal forces.

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power Competencies
competency_groups.description: Core competencies for Work, Energy and Power.
competencies: Understand the core concepts of Work, Energy and Power.
Apply key ideas from Work, Energy and Power to solve curriculum-aligned problems.
Analyze relationships between major concepts in Work, Energy and Power.

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rotational Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the motion of system of particles, rigid body dynamics, torque, angular momentum, equilibrium, moment of inertia, and comparison between linear and rotational motions.
competencies: Apply conservation of momentum principles to center of mass motion in a system of particles
Analyze relationships between torque, angular momentum, and their conservation laws
Evaluate conditions for rigid body equilibrium using equations of rotational motion
Differentiate linear and rotational kinematic quantities through comparative analysis
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering universal gravitation, variation in acceleration due to gravity, gravitational potential energy, orbital mechanics, and Kepler's laws.
competencies: Apply the Universal Law of Gravitation to calculate forces between celestial bodies
Determine variations in acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth
Calculate gravitational potential energy and potential for satellite systems
Derive escape speed and orbital velocity using conservation of energy principles

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Solids
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving elastic behavior, stress-strain relationships, material constants (Young's modulus, Bulk modulus), and energy storage in deformed solids.
competencies: Apply Hooke's Law to determine deformation limits for solid materials
Calculate Young’s modulus and Bulk modulus from experimental data or problem scenarios
Analyze the relationship between stress, strain, and elastic potential energy
Evaluate Poisson's ratio in the context of material deformation

competency_groups.title: Mechanical Properties of Fluids
competency_groups.description: Competencies addressing fluid dynamics, viscosity, surface tension, pressure variations in fluids, and applications like Bernoulli's theorem.
competencies: Apply Stokes' Law to determine terminal velocity of spherical bodies in viscous media
Distinguish between streamline and turbulent flow regimes using critical velocity concepts
Utilize Bernoulli’s Theorem to analyze fluid dynamics problems involving pressure, speed, and height
Calculate surface tension effects including excess pressure across curved surfaces

competency_groups.title: Thermal Properties of Matter
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to heat transfer mechanisms, specific heats, latent heat, thermal expansion anomalies, and blackbody radiation laws.
competencies: Explain the phenomenon of anomalous expansion in water and its physical significance
Apply calorimetry principles using specific heat capacities (Cp, Cv) for energy calculations
Analyze phase transitions involving latent heat during changes of state
Evaluate thermal conductivity effects across conduction, convection, and radiation modes

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics Fundamentals and Laws
competency_groups.description: Analyze thermal equilibrium, state variables, and the application of thermodynamic laws to gaseous processes.
competencies: Explain thermal equilibrium and apply Zeroth law concepts to define temperature.
Differentiate between heat, work, and internal energy using the First Law of Thermodynamics.
Analyze reversible/irreversible isothermal and adiabatic gaseous processes.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases and Molecular Properties
competency_groups.description: Derive macroscopic gas properties from microscopic molecular behavior using kinetic theory assumptions.
competencies: Explain the derivation of pressure and temperature based on kinetic theory assumptions.
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to determine specific heat capacities of gases.
Calculate RMS speed, mean free path, and utilize Avogadro's number in gas problems.
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Simple Harmonic Motion and Periodicity
competency_groups.description: Identify periodic functions, define Simple Harmonic Motion (S.H.M), and relate it to uniform circular motion.
competencies: Distinguish between general periodic motion and Simple Harmonic Motion using time period and frequency.
Formulate displacement as a function of time for S.H.M systems.
Relate the equations of Uniform Circular Motion to the dynamics of oscillations.

competency_groups.title: Wave Mechanics and Propagation
competency_groups.description: Understand the fundamental characteristics of wave motion, including propagation mechanisms and types.
competencies: Describe the basic properties and classification of mechanical waves.
Analyze the relationship between frequency, wavelength, and wave velocity in different media.
Explain the phenomenon of superposition and interference in wave systems.

competency_groups.title: Electric Charges and Fields Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding electric charges, fields, dipoles, and Gauss's theorem applications.
competencies: Apply Coulomb's law and superposition principle to calculate forces in charge systems.
Analyze the behavior of electric dipoles including torque in uniform electric fields.
Utilize Gauss's theorem to determine electric field intensity for symmetric charge distributions.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential and Capacitance Competencies
competency_groups.description: Exploring electric potential, equipotential surfaces, and capacitor properties.
competencies: Differentiate between conductors, insulators, and dielectrics regarding polarization effects.
Calculate equivalent capacitance for series and parallel combinations of capacitors.
Derive expressions for energy stored in a capacitor using standard formulae.

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity Competencies
competency_groups.description: Mastering concepts of current, resistance, emf, and circuit analysis.
competencies: Explain the relationship between drift velocity, mobility, and Ohm's law in conductors.
Analyze temperature dependence of resistivity and internal characteristics of cells.
Apply Kirchhoff's rules to solve complex circuits including Wheatstone bridge configurations.

competency_groups.title: Moving Charges and Magnetism Competencies
competency_groups.description: Investigating magnetic fields generated by currents and forces on moving charges.
competencies: Apply Biot-Savart law and Ampere's circuital law to calculate magnetic fields of current-carrying wires.
Determine the force exerted on a charge or conductor in an external magnetic field.
Understand the principle of converting a moving coil galvanometer into ammeters and voltmeters.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Magnetism and Matter Competencies
competency_groups.description: Examining properties of magnetic materials and their behavior in fields.
competencies: Classify magnetic materials based on qualitative properties such as susceptibility and permeability.
Model a bar magnet as an equivalent solenoid to explain its magnetic field lines.
Correlate the macroscopic magnetic moments of matter with atomic dipole alignments.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction Competencies
competency_groups.description: Mastering the principles of electromagnetic induction and its applications.
competencies: Explain Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction
Apply Lenz's Law to determine direction of induced current
Analyze self-inductance and mutual inductance phenomena
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current Competencies
competency_groups.description: Understanding the fundamentals of alternating currents in circuits.
competencies: Derive expressions for instantaneous and RMS values of AC
Analyze behavior of L, C, and R components in AC circuits
Calculate resonance frequency and Q-factor in series LC circuits
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves Competencies
competency_groups.description: Exploring the nature, properties, and spectrum of electromagnetic waves.
competencies: Describe the transverse nature of electromagnetic waves
Explain the relationship between electric and magnetic fields in EM waves
Identify regions of the electromagnetic spectrum based on frequency

competency_groups.title: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments Competencies
competency_groups.description: Applying laws of reflection, refraction, and optical instrument principles.
competencies: Apply Snell's Law to solve problems involving refraction at spherical surfaces
Derive the lens maker's formula for thin lenses
Explain the working principle of microscopes and telescopes

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics Competencies
competency_groups.description: Investigating wave phenomena such as interference, diffraction, and polarization.
competencies: Explain Young's double-slit experiment and derive fringe width formula
Analyze conditions for constructive and destructive interference
Describe the phenomenon of polarization and its applications

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.description: Understanding the photoelectric effect and wave-particle duality.
competencies: Explain the experimental evidence for the particle nature of light through the photoelectric effect.
Describe de Broglie's hypothesis regarding the wave nature of matter particles.
Analyze the relationship between photon energy, frequency, and work function.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms
competency_groups.description: Exploring atomic structure, models, and spectral series.
competencies: Evaluate Rutherford's alpha-particle scattering experiment and its implications for nuclear model.
Apply Bohr's postulates to explain the stability of atoms and hydrogen spectrum.
Identify different spectral series (Lyman, Balmer, Paschen) derived from electron transitions.

competency_groups.title: Nuclei
competency_groups.description: Investigating nuclear properties, binding energy, and reactions.
competencies: Calculate mass defect and binding energy per nucleon to explain nuclear stability.
Differentiate between nuclear fission and fusion processes with real-world examples.
Analyze the characteristics of radioactive decay laws and half-life.

competency_groups.title: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
competency_groups.description: Studying semiconductor physics and electronic device applications.
competencies: Distinguish between intrinsic and extrinsic semiconductors based on doping types.
Explain the working principles of p-n junction diodes in rectification circuits.
Design simple logic gate combinations using basic transistor switches.

activities.title: To make a paper scale of given least count, e.g., 0.2cm, 0.5 cm.
To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.
To plot a graph for a given set of data, with proper choice of scales and error bars.
To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.
To note the change in level of liquid in a container on heating and interpret the observations.
To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.
To study the factors affecting the rate of loss of heat of a liquid.
To study the effect of load on depression of a suitably clamped metre scale loaded at (i) its end (ii) in the middle.
To observe the decrease in pressure with increase in velocity of a fluid.
To measure the resistance and impedance of an inductor with or without iron core.
To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.
To assemble the components of a given electrical circuit.
To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.
To identify a diode, an LED, a resistor and a capacitor from a mixed collection of such items.
Use of multimeter to see the unidirectional flow of current in case of a diode and an LED and check whether a given electronic component (e.g., diode) is in working order.
To study effect of intensity of light (by varying distance of the source) on an LDR.
To observe refraction and lateral deviation of a beam of light incident obliquely on a glass slab.
To observe diffraction of light due to a thin slit.
To study the nature and size of the image formed by a (i) convex lens, or (ii) concave mirror, on a screen by using a candle and a screen (for different distances of the candle from the lens/mirror).
To obtain a lens combination with the specified focal length by using two lenses from the given set of lenses.

projects.title: To study various factors on which the internal resistance/EMF of a cell depends.

To study the variations in current flowing in a circuit containing an LDR because of a variation in (a) the power of the incandescent lamp, used to 'illuminate' the LDR (keeping all the lamps at a fixed distance). (b) the distance of an incandescent lamp (of fixed power) used to 'illuminate' the LDR.
To find the refractive indices of (a) water (b) oil (transparent) using a plane mirror, an equiconvex lens (made from a glass of known refractive index) and an adjustable object needle.
To investigate the relation between the ratio of (i) output and input voltage and (ii) number of turns in the secondary coil and primary coil of a self-designed transformer.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To investigate the dependence of the angle of deviation on the angle of incidence using a hollow prism filled one by one, with different transparent fluids.
To estimate the charge induced on each one of the two identical Styrofoam (or pith) balls suspended in a vertical plane by making use of Coulomb's law.

To study the factor on which the self-inductance of a coil depends by observing the effect of this coil, when put in series with a resistor/(bulb) in a circuit fed up by an A.C. source of adjustable frequency.
projects.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

projects.title: To study the earth's magnetic field using a compass needle -bar magnet by plotting magnetic field lines and tangent galvanometer.
projects.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

practicals.title: To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body and to measure internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using Vernier Callipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of a given wire and thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine volume of an irregular lamina using screw gauge.
To determine radius of curvature of a given spherical surface by a spherometer.
To determine the mass of two different objects using a beam balance.
To find the weight of a given body using parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum, plot its graph and use it to find the effective length of second's pendulum.
To study variation of time period of a simple pendulum of a given length by taking bobs of same size but different masses and interpret the result.
To study the relationship between force of limiting friction and normal reaction and to find the co- efficient of friction between a block and a horizontal surface.

To find the downward force, along an inclined plane, acting on a roller due to gravitational pull of the earth and study its relationship with the angle of inclination θ by plotting graph between force and Sinθ.
To determine Young's modulus of elasticity of the material of a given wire.
To find the force constant of a helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
To study the variation in volume with pressure for a sample of air at constant temperature by plotting graphs between P and V, and between P and 1/V.
practicals.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

practicals.title: To determine the surface tension of water by capillary rise method.
To determine the coefficient of viscosity of a given viscous liquid by measuring terminal velocity of a given spherical body.
To study the relationship between the temperature of a hot body and time by plotting a cooling curve.
To determine specific heat capacity of a given solid by method of mixtures.
To study the relation between frequency and length of a given wire under constant tension using sonometer.
To study the relation between the length of a given wire and tension for constant frequency using sonometer.
To find the speed of sound in air at room temperature using a resonance tube by two resonance positions.
To determine resistivity of two / three wires by plotting a graph for potential difference versus current.
To find resistance of a given wire / standard resistor using metre bridge.
To verify the laws of combination (series) of resistances using a metre bridge OR To verify the laws of combination (parallel) of resistances using a metre bridge.
To determine resistance of a galvanometer by half-deflection method and to find its figure of merit.

To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into a voltmeter of desired range and to verify the same OR To convert the given galvanometer (of known resistance and figure of merit) into an ammeter of desired range and to verify the same.
To find the frequency of AC mains with a sonometer.
To find the value of v for different values of u in case of a concave mirror and to find the focal length.
To find the focal length of a convex mirror, using a convex lens.
To find the focal length of a convex lens by plotting graphs between u and v or between 1/u and 1/v.
To find the focal length of a concave lens, using a convex lens.
To determine angle of minimum deviation for a given prism by plotting a graph between angle of incidence and angle of deviation.
To determine refractive index of a glass slab using a travelling microscope.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using convex lens and plane mirror.
To find the refractive index of a liquid using a concave mirror and a plane mirror.
To draw the I-V characteristic curve for a p-n junction diode in forward and reverse bias.
To measure diameter of a small spherical/cylindrical body using vernier calipers.
To measure the internal diameter and depth of a given beaker/calorimeter using vernier calipers and hence find its volume.
To measure diameter of given wire using screw gauge.
To measure thickness of a given sheet using screw gauge.
To determine the mass of a given object using a beam balance.
To find the weight of given body using the parallelogram law of vectors.
Using a simple pendulum plot L-T and graphs. Hence find the effective length of second's pendulum using appropriate length values.
To find the force constant of given helical spring by plotting a graph between load and extension.
